mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and remains elevated for at least one month, the restrictive monetary stance increases real borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 4.94% indicates restrictive regime
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rates above neutral
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment signals labor market deterioration and rising recession risk, triggering equity risk-off behavior and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors front-run earnings downgrades.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month-over-month by more than 50,000 persons
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 5-day SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales growth (RSAFS) combined with housing starts (HOUST) both declining simultaneously signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, indicating a demand-side recession that produces negative equity returns over 60 trading days as profit expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:current RSAFS at 364,198 million, HOUST at 1,849 thousand
regime_hint: demand-side recession or sharp demand contraction
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when both RSAFS and HOUST decline jointly, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above its 3-month trend while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains stable, signaling productive capacity expansion without demand-pull inflation, which supports margin expansion and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth exceeds its 3-month average by 0.2pp and CPIAUCSL month-over-month change is less than 0.3%
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 45% of triggered instances, or the joint signal shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.8% in a single month, signaling an abrupt deterioration in labor market tightness that increases recession probability and triggers equity de-risking, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market inflection point or shock
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in fewer than 52% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 4.8%, or the signal shows no statistical significance in a logistic regression (p > 0.20).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains above 2.5% annualized while housing starts (HOUST) exceed 1,800 thousand units, indicating sustained demand-side momentum and construction activity that supports corporate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current GDPC1 at 11,394.7 billion, HOUST at 1,849 thousand
regime_hint: any regime with sustained above-trend growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 48% of instances when both GDPC1 growth and HOUST levels are elevated, or the trend signal shows Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) decelerates month-over-month to below 0.2% while the federal funds rate remains above 4.5%, the real interest rate rises sharply, signaling monetary policy tightness that constrains equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between real rate proxies is not statistically significant (p > 0.12).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, signaling a supply-side shock combined with labor market weakness that compresses profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month and UNRATE rises month-over-month in the same month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 52% of instances when both INDPRO and UNRATE move adversely, or the joint signal shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.
